response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a full-stack web and software developer with over 15 years of experience in TypeScript, Node.js, and web infrastructure. While she demonstrates solid engineering fundamentals and some low-latency systems work, her background has no overlap with the core requirements of this role: SoC and IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture, or deep neural network workload optimization. The educational background (two bachelor's degrees, no master's or PhD) and the entirely software-focused skill set place her well outside the target profile for a Principal Performance Architect in hardware/AI accelerator infrastructure.
strengths: 15+ years of varied software engineering experience demonstrating adaptability across domains and tech stacks
Demonstrated performance engineering mindset in low-latency systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby using async I/O, caching, and high-performance databases)
Some embedded and hardware-adjacent exposure (C/C++ at National Instruments LabWindows, robotic arms at Altom)
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or hardware architecture experience of any kind
No AI, deep learning, or GPU workload optimization background despite recent AI-agent tooling work being application-level only
Educational profile (bachelor's degrees only, no advanced degree) does not meet the minimum qualification thresholds without compensating hardware specialization experience
